title: Veera Vinayaka Lyrics Vedalam Song Lyrics
stanzas:
Veera Vinayaka
Singer: Anirudh, Vishal Dadlani
Lyrics: Viveka
Movie: Vedalam
Ganapathi baapaa moriya..

[
Veera vinayaka
vettri vinayaka
shakthi vinayaka
paerazhaga

Theera sandhosamum
thiththikkum vaarthaiyum
eththikkum thondrida
venumaiyya
] (2)

Eesan pettra aasai maganae
eedu inayae illa thunaiyae

Naadu nagaram sezhikkum,
unnai naadi vandhor
vazhakai uyarum

hey nee poodhu velaasu
nee poondhu velaasu
kondaadu idhu urchaaga neram.

hey nee vuttu velaasu
vaa vuttu velaasu
kondaadu ini kooththaadum
kaalam..

enrichedMetadata.faq:
Frequently Asked Questions

What is the meaning of the song 'Veera Vinayaka'?

'Veera Vinayaka' is a devotional song that praises Lord Ganesha, celebrating his strength and blessings.

How does this song fit into the plot of the movie 'Vedalam'?

The song is featured during a pivotal moment in Vedalam, emphasizing themes of devotion and strength in the face of challenges.

Who are the singers of 'Veera Vinayaka'?

The song is sung by Anirudh and Vishal Dadlani, both known for their powerful vocal performances.

When was 'Veera Vinayaka' released?

'Veera Vinayaka' was released on October 15, 2015.
enrichedMetadata.summary: 'Veera Vinayaka' is a spirited devotional song that captures the essence of faith and celebration. In the context of the movie Vedalam, it serves as a powerful anthem that uplifts the characters and the audience alike. The song conveys a sense of joy and reverence, inviting listeners to embrace the strength of Lord Ganesha. With its energetic beats and heartfelt lyrics, it evokes a feeling of unity and celebration, making it perfect for festive occasions.
enrichedMetadata.high_ctr_intro: Veera Vinayaka from Vedalam is a vibrant celebration of devotion that brings together the powerful vocals of Anirudh and Vishal Dadlani. This energetic track is sure to uplift your spirits and resonate with the festive vibes. Experience the magic of this unique collaboration that perfectly blends traditional themes with contemporary sounds.
